purpose: Interpretable (glass-box) document vectorizer (gram2vec; arXiv:2406.12131): every dimension is a HUMAN-NAMED stylometric feature — function words, character n-grams, punctuation, paragraph/dialogue shape, pronoun/modal/negation rates — emitted as a named-feature coordinate (vector + vector_flat) in a deterministic family-then-name order, so a human can read the difference dimension by dimension. A VECTORIZER, not a classifier: there is NO aggregate scalar at all (no overall distance, no score), so there is structurally nothing to threshold or rank on. The feature math is REUSED verbatim from stylometry_core.extract_features (include_spacy=False), not re-implemented. With an optional --baseline-dir / --manifest it adds a per-dimension reference distribution (mean/sd/n) and a PROVISIONAL descriptive band (mean ± k·sd) — illustrative, not a verdict; held-out disjoint (warns if the target is a baseline member). NOT a verdict: not authorship/AI, not same-author, not a writing-quality score, and not endorsed as a classifier/selection/training target. Claim-license refuses those readings; thresholds operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You want each named stylometric feature's value, by name, as a readable coordinate
You want a transparent companion to voice_profile / voice_distance with no opaque embedding
You want a per-dimension position against a held-out writer/register baseline (provisional band)
do_not_use_when: You want an authorship/AI verdict, a same-author claim, or a writing-quality judgment
You want a single aggregate distance/score (use voice_distance — this surface emits none)
Non-English text (the function-word list and char-n-gram axes are English-tuned)

compute.cost_note: stdlib only (M1); reuses stylometry_core feature builders with include_spacy=False.

examples.description: Named-feature vector of a document (single mode, full inventory)
Per-dimension position against a held-out baseline (provisional band)

references: gram2vec: An Interpretable Document Vectorizer (arXiv:2406.12131): https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.12131
